sections.paragraph: m. pl. i.q. מֹורִגִּים threshing wains, instruments which were used for treading out corn; made of thick timber, and having the lower side armed and jagged with iron or fire-stones [flints], which abound in Palestine, so that the corn was rubbed out. This word appears to be derived from the unused word בַּרְקָן giving out light, which I expect denoted the fire-stone (comp. بَرْقَةُ stony ground, perh. prop. abounding in fire-stone, as is the case with a great part of Palestine and Arabia); whence בַּרְקָנִי a threshing wain armed with fire-stones; pl. בַּרְקָנִים. It occurs twice, Jud. 8:7 , 16 Jdg 8:16.
